title: papaver orientale var. oreophilum
intro: Papaver orientale var. oreophilum, also known as the Oriental Poppy, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its vibrant red or purple flowers and delicate, papery petals. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids, such as meconic acid and papaverine. The health benefits of the Oriental Poppy include reducing pain and inflammation, relieving anxiety and stress, and promoting better sleep. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
